race.name: Granfondo Belgium
race.tagline: 138km of Ardennes fury with 2100m punch—car-free UCI qualifier that bites back.

vitals.location: Les Lacs de l'Eau d'Heure, Hainaut, Belgium
vitals.registration: €60-€95 includes bib, goodie bag, medal, certificate, medical aid, UCI quals
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple feed stations plus finish drinks
route_options: Gran Fondo: 85.7mi, 6890ft — UCI flagship with lead-in + 4x24km laps

climate.description: March in Wallonia delivers 5-10°C temps with frequent rain and gusty winds that turn climbs into slogs. Muddy roads and chill demand full kit. Annual swings keep it unpredictable—pack for misery.

terrain.surface: Fully paved, car-free roads reward smooth power. Lakeside flats mix with relentless rollers. Border hills deliver grippy, technical bite without gravel distractions.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 67. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 63.

course_description.character: Ardennes microcosm: 33km opener splits the pack on 3km@3% (8%max) before 4x24km laps south to Couvin. Lakeside calm yields to valley crushers. Paved, rolling brutality tests pacing over raw distance.
course_description.signature_challenge: Fourfold Petit Poggio laps forge unbreakable engines amid repeat suffering. Cumulative 2100m demands fueling mastery. Car-free flow lets pain purify.

logistics.airport: Brussels (BRU) 1.5hrs north; Charleroi (CRL) closer for budget flights. Drive south via E42. French border proximity aids Paris hubs.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Base in Philippeville or Couvin for lakeside calm. Campsites near Plate Taille for immersion. Book early—limited spots.
logistics.food: Lakeside cafes fuel pre-race; post-ride frites and beer await. Feeds include essentials—pack gels for laps.

history.origin_story: Born 2025 as UCI qualifier amid Eau d'Heure's serene lakes and Franco-Belgian hills. Turbulent debut sparked total 2026 overhaul: car-free roads, safety upgrades. Now a selective spring beast qualifying top age-groupers for worlds.
notable_moments: UCI series entry with border hills
history.reputation: Rapid redemption arc from chaos to polished UCI contender. Riders rave safety gains. Building buzz as Wallonia's qualifier king.

biased_opinion.summary: Granfondo Belgium 2.0 torches its turbulent debut with car-free roads and selective savagery around Eau d'Heure's emerald basins. 138km of Ardennes pain qualifies elites while capping chaos at 2k riders. UCI prestige meets Belgian grit—spring sharpeners, this is your qualifier ticket. Laps grind souls but scenery soothes; post-2025 glow makes it unmissable.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Early UCI gem with legs for glory. Nail training, dodge rain—bag that top-25%. Skip if hating cold puddles.

final_verdict.one_liner: Car-free Ardennes qualifier punches way above rookie weight.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if chasing UCI worlds quals with Ardennes appetite and rainproof grit. Skip if spring slop or lap monotony kills your vibe—wait for field maturity.
final_verdict.alternatives: Haute Route classics for vert overload (haute-route-slug), Flanders cobbles for pro vibes (tour-of-flanders-sportive), Lombardy crushers (granfondo-lombardia)

youtube_data.search_query: Granfondo Belgium gran fondo cycling Les Lacs de l'Eau d'Heure
